alternative_parents.description: 5-alpha-steroids carrying a C=O group at the 3-position of the steroid skeleton.
alternative_parents.name: 3-oxo-5-alpha-steroids

alternative_parents.description: Steroids with a structure characterized a C=O group at the 3-position and a nitrogen atom replacing the carbon atom at the 4-position.
alternative_parents.name: 3-oxo-4-azasteroids

alternative_parents.description: Steroid derivatives carrying a C=O group at the 17-position of the steroid skeleton.
alternative_parents.name: 17-oxosteroids

alternative_parents.description: Azasteroids where the carbon atom at position 4 is replaced by a nitrogen atom.
alternative_parents.name: 4-azasteroids and derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a quinolidine or decahydroquinoline moiety, which is a bicyclic skeleton consisting of a piperidine fused to a cyclohexane ring.
alternative_parents.name: Quinolidines

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a piperidine ring which bears a ketone.
alternative_parents.name: Piperidinones

alternative_parents.description: Cyclic organic compounds containing a piperidin-2-one moiety.
alternative_parents.name: Delta lactams

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a secondary carboxylic acid amide functional group, with the general structure RC(=O)N(R')H (R,R'=alkyl, aryl).
alternative_parents.name: Secondary carboxylic acid amides

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds  in which a carbonyl group is bonded to two carbon atoms R2C=O (neither R may be a hydrogen atom). Ketones that have one or more alpha-hydrogen atoms undergo keto-enol tautomerization, the tautomer being an enol.
alternative_parents.name: Ketones

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an heterocycle with at least one nitrogen atom and one carbon atom linked to each other.
alternative_parents.name: Azacyclic compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a nitrogen atom.
alternative_parents.name: Organonitrogen compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an oxide group.
alternative_parents.name: Organic oxides

alternative_parents.description: Derivatives of hydrocarbons obtained by substituting one or more carbon atoms by an heteroatom. They contain at least one carbon atom and heteroatom.
alternative_parents.name: Hydrocarbon derivatives

class.description: Compounds based on the cyclopenta[a]phenanthrene carbon skeleton, partially or completely hydrogenated; there are usually methyl groups at C-10 and C-13, and often an alkyl group at C-17. By extension, one or more bond scissions, ring expansions and/or ring contractions of the skeleton may have occurred.
class.name: Steroids and steroid derivatives

description: This compound belongs to the class of organic compounds known as androgens and derivatives. These are 3-hydroxylated C19 steroid hormones. They are known to favor the development of masculine characteristics. They also show profound effects on scalp and body hair in humans.

direct_parent.description: 3-hydroxylated C19 steroid hormones. They are known to favor the development of masculine characteristics. They also show profound effects on scalp and body hair in humans.
direct_parent.name: Androgens and derivatives

kingdom.description: Compounds that contain at least one carbon atom, excluding isocyanide/cyanide and their non-hydrocarbyl derivatives, thiophosgene, carbon diselenide, carbon monosulfide, carbon disulfide, carbon subsulfide, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, Carbon suboxide, and dicarbon monoxide.
kingdom.name: Organic compounds

subclass.description: Steroids with a structure based on the 19-carbon androstane skeleton.
subclass.name: Androstane steroids

superclass.description: Fatty acids and their derivatives, and substances related biosynthetically or functionally to these compounds.
superclass.name: Lipids and lipid-like molecules
